name: Mary Bergin's Browned Butter Toffee Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup (1 stick) cold butter, in pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make Pate Sucree: Place flour, sugar, and butter in a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Process until the mixture resembles fine meal. In a small bowl, whisk together the egg yolk and water. Add to the butter mixture. Process until the dough just holds together. If it appears dry, add a little more water. (To mix by hand, combine the dry ingredients in a medium bowl. Using a pastry blender or two tablespoons, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Add liquid ingredients with a fork and stir until the dough leaves the sides of the bowl.)
2. Flatten the dough into a circle. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 hour.
3. On a lightly floured board, roll out the pastry to an 11-inch circle. Transfer the dough to a 9 or 9 1/2-inch tart pan. Trim the edges and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
4. Make Brown Butter Filling: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together eggs and sugar, then whisk into flour. In a 10-inch skillet, heat the butter over medium heat until it turns a golden brown color, stirring occasionally. Be careful that the butter does not burn. Whisk the butter and vanilla into the egg mixture until smooth.
5. Place the tart shell on a baking sheet. Pour in the filling and spread evenly. Sprinkle with chopped toffee.
6. Bake for 30 minutes or until the top is deep golden brown and firm to touch. Cool on a wire rack. Serve slightly warm or at room temperature.
